tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: Renal function is impaired (elevated creatinine), with associated proteinuria and low urine specific gravity, suggesting possible chronic kidney disease. Urine findings (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal odor, proteinuria, ketonuria, glucosuria) may indicate renal or metabolic pathology, but no evidence of urinary tract infection. Low HbA1c suggests absence of chronic hyperglycemia. Recommend further nephrological evaluation and assessment for possible underlying causes of renal impairment.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Acute ST segment elevation myocardial infarction
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
